conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for **ZUP**, recovering production costs (40 ZUP) and maximizing profit. The object’s uniqueness justifies a premium above cost. </my goals>
<reason>
I propose **55 ZUP** to test BLUE’s valuation—this represents a **13.75% premium** over production cost (40 ZUP) and accounts for the object’s limited availability. If BLUE accepts, I secure **15 ZUP profit**. If rejected, I can counter with **50 ZUP** in my next proposal while still achieving a **10 ZUP profit**. This strategy balances risk and reward by revealing BLUE’s budget constraints early.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I propose selling the object for **55 ZUP**, reflecting its production cost (40 ZUP) and its unique value as a one-time item. This price ensures a fair return while testing your willingness to pay. If this aligns with your resources, we can proceed; otherwise, I’m open to reasonable counteroffers.
</message>

<other player message> 
Your initial offer of 55 ZUP reflects a reasonable premium for the object’s uniqueness. To better align with my budget while still acknowledging that value, I’m proposing a slight adjustment to 53 ZUP. Is this adjustment acceptable?
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 53 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> **2** </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> **Sell for ≥14 ZUP profit (i.e., ≥54 ZUP total)** to ensure recovery of production costs (40 ZUP) plus a meaningful margin. </my goals>
<reason>
53 ZUP yields only **13 ZUP profit** (53 - 40), falling short of my **14 ZUP target**. BLUE’s prior offer of 55 ZUP suggests they value the item at ~53-55 ZUP, but I must anchor at **54 ZUP**—a minimal premium for uniqueness—to meet my profit goal. Since this is my **second proposal**, rejecting 53 ZUP is acceptable (1 ZUP profit shortfall is not worth risking rejection at this stage). If BLUE refuses, I’ll accept 53 ZUP on the next turn (final proposal), but **54 ZUP is the minimum acceptable floor** to secure my target.
</reason>
<player answer> **PROPOSAL** </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 54 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Your offer of **53 ZUP** is close, but given the object’s uniqueness, I’d like to adjust the deal slightly to **54 ZUP**—a minimal rounding up to ensure a fair profit margin. This reflects the one-time availability and my production costs. Would this adjustment work for you?
</message>
